string.text: Viktor
Cherry
Dima
Atreo
Militar
Vovan
Leto
Stalker
PDA recording
Monolith Soldier
Duty Soldier
Bandit
Bandits
Trodnik
Trodnik's PDA
Radio
I shouldn't go there...
I can't go back now. I have to end this once and for all.
You’re finally awake, stalker. Come on, let’s talk for a bit.
Looks like it fits you well. I thought it might be too big for you... Alright then, follow me and don’t stray too far.
Alright. I’ll follow you.
Hope the light didn’t blind you.
Look, my friend. Come closer. From here you can see them resting: a pack of fleshes.
...Fleshes? What are those?
Get ready, Viktor. Hold the grip tight or it’ll snap your wrist. If you need to reload, pull the latch back from the barrel and load in a few shells.
But what the hell are fleshes?
What the hell were those things? Were they pigs? They were all deformed!

Yeah, and that’s not the only ugly thing you’ll see around here. The place is crawling with nasty bandits too... If you’ve got the stomach for it, you can go up with your knife and carve out their eyes or some meat. sometimes stalkers buy that stuff. I’m going to check the area—don’t you dare lower your guard.
Listen, I found a trail and it’s definitely not from the fleshes we just killed. Follow me.
They’ve set up a camp, and I think I know who it was...
Who? More fleshes?!
No. those things don’t pitch tents. (laughs)
I knew it. That campfire was used recently. Viktor, get ready. The bandits are up to something—we’re going to pay them a visit.
Bandits!? I didn’t realize we were dealing with people. What the hell do we do?
Let me go in first. Stay put and wait for one of them to show up—then take the shot. After that, whatever we find is yours.
Alright. I’ve got you covered. I’ll stay right behind you.
My wrist hurts... and my hands are shaking. I’ve never killed anyone before.

The shaking will pass in a few minutes... it happens to all of us. If it helps, they would’ve done far worse to you if they’d had the chance, bandits show no mercy. Anyway, let’s talk for a moment. I found something interesting.
Hey! You’re Viktor, right? Welcome to the Zone. Viper gave me your contact. Meet me at the house west of the Rookie Village.
Watch out for bandits.
Viktor! I managed to get the box open. Swing by and we’ll talk.
Viktor, I’ve got some information that might be really useful to you. Stop by and we’ll talk it over.
You must be the new guy. Vinicio, right? Make yourself comfortable. Let’s have a good chat.
The name's Viktor
Yeah, whatever. Viktor, come in.
Go on, don’t worry about it. There’s a bit of an irregularity in the readings among those crates and bits of scrap metal. Just stand right there.
Right here, correct?
Excellent! Right where I said it would be. Despite the radiation, you’re handling it remarkably well.
Yes, yes. Where do I go now!?
See the remains of that crane cabin up ahead? Yes, right there. That’s the spot.
Good. No time to poke around in stashes—just ignore everything or the radiation’s going to leave you bald.
Hurry up! Where the hell am I supposed to go now!?
Head for the outlet of that concrete-covered steel pipe. Check around there, alright? Hurry up.
Hmm... the radiation levels are a bit higher than normal. You’d better run. The next spot is right behind you—near that old, rusted truck cargo.
So... Where now!?
Oh, I thought I’d already said it. On the ground, circle around the tree to your right and head over there.
Alright, I’ll mark the next one on your PDA. It’s a bit tricky to explain. You’ll need to get close to the anomalies... so be careful.
Ok, there's just 10 remaining spots.
Just kidding, bro. Get out of there already. Come over and I’ll give you some medkits and a well-deserved reward.
Hey, look. There goes the one who killed the Mage.
There goes the guy who beat you to that fanatic job. Heard he’s quick.”
Psst, look. That’s the stalker who’s been climbing rooftops.
Hey! Thanks for dealing with the mutant in the tunnel. I’d been meaning to head to the swamps for a while now...
There goes Viktor. Thanks for keeping the village safe, brother.
Isn’t that the one who saved you the other day?
Isn’t that the guy Hook’s been looking for?
That’s the guy Viper was dragging around earlier...
Hey, Viktor’s right behind you. No—don’t look! He’ll see you!
Is that guy carrying a damn pickaxe in his backpack? Hey... going artifact hunting or mining?
Look, that’s the one I told you about—the guy who found all that food... Lucky bastard.

Tornillo’s PDA... Let’s see what’s on it. Looks like he was doing some light research on the safest spots to find low-tier artifacts. It’s also full of photos from his time in the Zone — a few of them with his friend Cabo... Among the deleted files, there’s a note... It says: “It’s Kolyan. He’s the one who had me killed.” Who the hell is that? I need to figure out what to do with this PDA — it’s got valuable info. But Cabo deserves to see it too.

I found a note in the stash... It reads: "If you’re reading this, then your dreams have guided you well. Your psychic sensitivity is above average — that’s why you were able to find this place before the others. Here, you’ll find what you need: the first Key, an artifact that will resonate with you and lead you to the other four." what?

"Together, the five will reveal a power that transcends all limits... but you’re not ready to understand it yet. As you move forward, the veil between your mind and what dwells above the Zone will grow thinner. Then I will be able to show you more. Follow the pulse. Do not stop, Nox." but there was no artifact there. I need to dig deeper — someone got here before me.

This PDA belongs to someone named "Sentinel". There are a few entries from a couple of days ago: "My leg wound is getting infected... I need a loan to pay for these damn antibiotics." Damn, poor guy. There’s another entry a few days later: “There’s this guy, Atreo. Came out of nowhere, says he can give me the money I need if I go with him to the swamps. Of course I said yes — even if I’m limping and it hurts, it beats losing my damn leg.” Guess it didn’t end well. That explains why the corpse was limping.

An old sheet of paper, covered in notes passed between different people. The last entries read: "Couldn’t get in. There were too many of them, coming from every direction — looks like they’re marching south in massive numbers, fresh brains all nicely washed. My rifle broke from overuse, so I’m leaving it in the stash to fix it later. Dante, I’m gonna need that blue tape of yours — the ‘fix anything’ kind. Leaving you some supplies in return." The reply is written below in neat handwriting: "Came back wrecked from the Forest. I’m taking the supplies you left, and I’ll grab your Vintar to repair it at the workbench in the cemetery. Tank asked me to leave some backup weapons, so I’ll stash them in box #13, above the tunnel leading to Darkscape. Leaving some pills here too — you’ll need them. Until next time. —Dante" So we’ve got Dante... and another stalker.

Another worn-out piece of paper — looks like it’s passed through a lot of hands. The last note reads: "Hook, Tank, Vera — I saw a rookie trying to loot this stash. Looks like the seasonal shift exposed it. I’m moving everything to stash #7 and abandoning this one. Leaving one of the keys. Stay safe." Below it, written in cursive: "Goddamn it, Dante... always playing mysterious, moving stashes around, like we’ve got time to keep searching and searching. I left a really important mag here and now it’s gone. Lost my map with all the stash locations too. I don’t know which damn one is #7 — was it the boxcar on the bridge, the one over the Pripyat River... or the one in Darkscape!? Damn it, man... I swear I’m gonna knock your teeth in when I find you." The last note isn’t signed. But now we’ve got more names: Dante, Hook, Vera, and Tank.

A crumpled ball of paper. Once unfolded, there’s a note inside: "Saw some people who used to be Clear Sky in Cordon. Are they planning something? Couldn’t stick around, but I’m leaving this warning so it doesn’t catch you off guard. Tank, thanks a lot for the artifact — makes carrying all this weight a lot easier. Left a few goodies in return. Stay safe, see you at the Bar." Below it, in different handwriting: "Guys, Monolith forces reached Cordon and sealed off the north. Looks like Duty is trying to hold them back, but there were a LOT of them. I don’t think they’ll last, so I’m heading through the swamps. I’ll try to leave intel at the pumping station, up top — you know where. Be careful... I heard they killed the trader Mark recently." Yeah... things are getting bad.

A note scribbled on a napkin: "Guys, I came to the swamps to check on the Clear Sky situation. Looks like they’re making a comeback… They’re few, but well-armed, and I’ve seen them moving north at night. During the day, they keep a low profile — probably to avoid trouble with the military. I’ll have to go through Darkscape. Hope my mag is still in the rocks behind the gas station... or we’re gonna have a problem :) P.S. Took all the supplies. Left Dante’s key so he doesn’t start whining." Looks like this guy’s still pissed about his magazines... I need to check the next stash.

A slightly more carefully handled piece of paper than the others, written neatly and with patience: "Dante, Hook — I ran into Tank and we headed toward the Hospital together. Hope to see you at the bar! Miss you guys. -Vera" Another, much messier handwriting adds: "YOU BETTER BRING MY MAGAZINES TO THE BAR, GOT IT? VERA WANTS TO SEE YOU. I’LL BE WAITING THERE. I’LL LEAVE SUPPLIES ON TOP OF THE VALLEY BRIDGE — BUT KNOW I’M DOING IT VERY PISSED OFF. — TANKATHLETE, THE BEST STALKER IN THE ZONE" Well... looks like he never got his magazines. Poor Tank.

A note scribbled on a diet sausage wrapper... It reads: "I fixed the rifle, Hook. But I can’t leave it here—the Valley’s too hot right now. You know how it is... I’ll stash it at the Truck Cemetery. I’m leaving the treasure hunter to help you find it, since our old stash there got wrecked by mutants. You know how it works. — Dante" Could this be the rifle they mentioned in the first note? I need to find it.

Another note, this one written in a hurry. The message is short: "I checked with the Doctor about the artifacts. The PSI-manipulation one is with Leto—seems he doesn’t really know how to use it, and Monolith is tracking him. The regeneration one is with someone named Kolyan. I don’t know him, but he sounds like a bandit with some serious pull. That leaves us with two more to find, not counting ours. I’ll see you at the Hospital—let’s hope your lead checks out, Hook. — Dante" Signed by Dante... I need to track these guys down.

Neat, almost calligraphic handwriting reads: "I came through again! But this time I got ahead of him! Managed to steal his stuff before he even noticed — what a complete idiot. It’s unbelievable they still fall for it every time I switch gas masks… stalkers are getting sloppier by the day." Below the note, there’s a messy, angry face scribbled: >:( The handwriting doesn’t match the calm script above...Was that someone else?

The Mage’s PDA... It’s locked. The only thing visible is a list of recent messages from the following contacts: Leto, Teeth, Kolyan, Sultan, Key, Borov, Bartender... Looks like he had some powerful connections. I should take this to Wolf.

A note on clean paper — unusually well-kept for the Zone: "Listen, Mage. I had to send a few of these idiots to camp near your position. I told them to wait for you, and they’ve got orders to join your cause when you’re ready to carry out the plan. There should be nine bandits — if fewer made it, ask them what the hell happened. I don’t want any of them hiding out in Cordon, or we’ll draw attention." — Pirate

In the photo, there are two people wearing light blue and white camouflage suits... I’ve never seen gear like that in the Zone. They’re posing together, making hand signs at the camera. They look like good friends... The one on the right feels familiar.
I should take this to Dogo.
Viper’s gonna be happy with what I found.
Rooster’s gonna pay me well for all the running around I had to do...
The One Who Knows All repeated it a hundred thousand times: wait at these coordinates until the heretic shows himself. It’s been six days now, carrying out the will of the Monolith.

We tracked a stalker who ran past us wounded. Saw him stop in the tunnel to catch his breath. We’re moving in... The guys are losing it. the stalker in the yellow jacket made some kind of hand signs and everything went to hell... I had to shoot them — they were attacking me. My head’s about to explode... I’m seeing double, colors all wrong… everything turns red sometimes, then blue... If anyone finds this PDA, please get it to Cold.
Alright, we’re here. You go up through that window — cover me from the rooftop in case anything happens.
Hold position, Viktor. Everything’s going to be fine.
You’re not the bald guy! Where’s Fanatic?
That’s me — bald under this helmet. (laughs) But you’re not Serdyuk... what the hell’s going on?
Open fire! We need those frequencies!
Shoot, Viktor!
Shit! Take cover!
Rover, Selco — take the one on the roof! The rest of you, surround the bald guy!
Well... looks like I got sold out. Guess I put too much faith in military “goodwill” Come here, son. Let’s talk before we head back to base.
I know you’re good at stealth, so I won’t spell everything out. Head to the fence — stay close to it to avoid the landmines. You’ll see a fallen tree ahead, that’s your way in.
One more thing — don’t turn on your flashlight, or the sniper will spot you.
Good. Climb over the tree. I’ll keep an eye on the situation from here.

I see three of them on patrol — not too many. One’s moving back and forth between the office and the drop-off point for the documents. Another’s patrolling the parking lot near the barracks. The last one’s walking the street... You’ll need to go up the stairs and leave the documents on the desk — without being seen.
Perfect. Wait for an opening, then head back the way you came. You can climb onto the roof to get over the fence.
Son, I have good news. Come see me before you leave.
The Monolith was right... you’ve come.
Listen... I haven’t set foot in the Great Swamps in years. Got a lot of history with that place — none of it good. We go in careful.
Got it. So... history, huh? What happened there?
Long story, friend. Real long. I’ll tell you another time, when we’re not on edge like this.
Fine. You owe me that story.
I’ll cover you. Cross the fence and follow the tracks. I’ll stay behind, make sure no one’s on our tail.
Where do those tracks lead?
Head east, they’ll take you back to Cordon. Past a tunnel, they lead into Darkscape... no man’s land.
And to the west?
About a kilometer out, there are railway ruins torn apart by anomalies. Then there’s a bridge crossing the Pripyat River.
A bridge, huh… interesting.
I know you’re eager to explore the Zone, Viktor. But don’t get lost thinking ahead. Let’s stay focused. I don’t want to spend a second longer here than we have to.
One important thing: don’t go through the water. The radiation will poison you bad.
Viktor, better stay out of the grass. You’ll make noise, and that’s the kind of attention we don’t want.
Hold up a second, friend. Since we’re here, let me make a quick stop.
Sure. What is it?
Rest in peace, friends...
Alright, let’s move.
We’re almost there.
Too quiet... I don’t like it.
What the hell is that!?
Shoot! It’s not human anymore!
I’ll stand watch. Take your time checking things out...
Alright, give me a minute.
No clues on the bodies. Sidorovich’s frequencies are missing, and there’s no sign of Atreo... I don’t get what happened.

We’ll have to ask around in Cordon if anyone’s seen Atreo. Let’s head back through the smuggler tunnel. We’ll use the cover of night so the military doesn’t gun us down. Let me know when you’re ready.
(Whispering) Shhh… this one’s mine. Turn off your flashlight and keep quiet.
Didn’t know you had a silenced weapon...
They’re necessary. I’d recommend getting one... (sigh) Let’s get moving.
No! Turn it off-
Well, Viktor — good run. No major trouble. Nicely done. I’m gonna go see a friend now. You’ll probably want to check in with Sidorovich. Good luck — come find me if you need anything.
(Whistles, calling the dogs) Good… that worked.
Oh no! Here they come! Don’t let them get close!
Damn filthy dogs!
Dont be a pussy, Help me!
Ah, for fuck’s sake... I thought you’d be more useful. Ended up doing most of the work myself.
Idiot...
Monolith... I see them...
I see heretics dressed in red and black...
Infidels ahead!
Destroy them in the name of the Monolith!
Follow the Monolith’s will!
Leave no one alive.
The Monolith commands us to fire before they can react.
FOR THE MONOLITH!
For the monolith!
They’re flanking us from the hill!
Take cover!
Young man, come speak with me before you leave. We have unfinished business.
B.S.C. to Garbage, B.S.C. to Garbage — do you copy?
B.S.C. to Garbage, B.S.C. to Garbage — come in?
Oh, yeah. I don’t have the artifact on me — follow me for a minute.
(Laughs)
Wow. Surprised you’re still conscious. But you’re not getting out of this. Nobody survives this...
What’s happening!? What is this!?
¡ATREUS!
Hey, friend! Welcome back. Do you remember what happened? Talk to me.
What the fuck!? HEY! Who are you!? I’ll shoot!
So Leto went ahead and dealt with those stalkers on the way... Poor bastards. I should go see Viper — he’ll know how to track him down.
Alright. Got my gear. Let’s move!
Pay attention, stalker. You’re about to see an expert at work.
(Humming) When we find Atreo, I’m gonna gouge his fucking eyes out.
Rookie, heads up. Grab your stuff — we’re moving.
All set, sir! Ready!
Dogo! You can bring Linda out now — don’t forget the ammo crate.
Yeah... time to take Linda out for a walk.
Rooster! You’re forgetting the medkits again...
Huh, could’ve sworn they were in my backpack. (laughs)
Viktor! I can see the excitement in your eyes. Relax. We’ll find him.
(Embarrassed) I’m fine, don’t worry. (laughs)
(Mocking a Monolith soldier) “For the Monolith... attack the one in Duty colors....”
Rooster! FOR FUCK’S SAKE! I thought you were a Monolith soldier!
(Laughter)
Can’t believe you fall for that every time.
You should relax a little, Rookie..
Enough! Stay sharp. Monolith could show up any second. Especially you, Rookie — you scare easy as it is.
Buzzkill...
(Whispering) Stalker... I heard you needed a suit. Come closer, let’s talk.
Noise on the roof! GO CHECK IT OUT!
Kid, we found the thief. Come by when you can — we need to talk about that bastard.
Please, take care of my boys, kid.

Kid, I’ve been going through the Mage’s PDA and found some things. Looks like he made a deal with some bandits to attack the rookie village... I’m counting on you — come see me when you can and I’ll explain everything.
Good work. Let’s find the next group.
Let’s move on to the next group.
That should be all of them... The village is safe now. Come here — I’ve got something for you.
Rookie Fedya, routine equipment check tonight. Don’t forget to bring your weapon.
Yes, Captain! I’ll have my gear ready for tonight.
You’re in deep shit, Cabo...
Viktor, look... (whispering) Please — find my weapon. I’ll pay you well.
No... these photos aren’t even a month old. And now I’ll never see him again...
No, seriously. It’s strange Tornillo would slip up over something as basic as radiation. Something feels off... Am I missing something here? ...No. Leave it.
You fucking pig... tough bastard.
Rookie, there’s burnt fuzz in that tunnel. Stay crouched and don’t make any sudden moves.
Relax, stalker. I was joking — I wasn’t about to sprint all the way back to base...
(Agitado) Hijo de puta... (Risas)
You know, this tunnel used to have electrical anomalies. One day a scientist showed up, set up some devices, an artifact appeared... and the anomalies vanished for good.
(Aún agitado) Interesante...
Listen, rookie. Thanks for the help — I think you’ll make a fine stalker someday. Those ghosts really messed with my head. Come on, let’s talk about the pay you earned before I pass out.
Don’t you feel something off? I hear... a buzzing.
It’s them! Those are the ghosts that attacked me!
Agh! My head! I can hear their voices!
STALKER! UP AHEAD! To the NORTH — I saw something, I swear!
Stalker, don’t stray too far from me. Just making sure nothing happens to you...
Rookie, move your ass!
Move!
Ugh... Rookie. Move!
Rookie... (Impatient)
Rooster! Don’t fall behind — you’re looking way too relaxed.
Yeah, yeah, I’m coming, Captain Viper.
Stay off the road. Let’s take the path on the right and use the trees for cover.
¡Got it!
You saw them, right? There’s several. Keep your heads down and move...
Heh. Keep my head down? Screw that. Hey Monoliths! I’m right here — come to papa!
Dogo, don’t let that happen again. We’re moving quietly for more than one reason. Follow the instructions.
What’s with all the protocol? It’s not like there’s a sniper or a machine gun nest up ahead.
Sniper, one o’clock — ahead!
I see him. Take cover! Viktor — get over here. You’re up.
¡Im going!
Covering fire! Move, Viktor! To the tree on your left — stay there until he reloads!
Good! Hold there until he reloads...
Nice! Stay put — then move to your left.
You’re doing great, Viktor! Next, run to the tree on your right!
Careful, kid. Don’t cross Linda’s line of fire unless you want to lose your legs.
Good, friend! Hold there — then zigzag to the tree on your left.
Whew... that was close! Nice!
Viktor! He’s exposed! You’ve got a clear shot — he’s all yours!
Incredible... let’s hope there aren’t more of them.
...We could really use a sniper right about now.
(Mocking) Take this seriously, Dogo — or you’ll end up like that sniper.
I hate snipers. Bunch of damn cowards.
Oh yeah? And who’s gonna take me down? Some egghead like you?
Enough. Let’s move along the edge of the road heading east.
Careful — there’s a damn fire anomaly in the middle of the road.
HAHAHA! Did you see that Monolith drop!? Stiff as a board! HA!
Stay sharp. We’ll move up to that helicopter ahead and take a breather there. I need to patch myself up — I’m losing blood...
I’ll give you a hand. Got some bandages from the scientists... Well, “from the scientists” — they’re just regular bandages with their patches on them. Still do the job.
Alright... let’s rest a bit. Rookie, take watch — I’ll relieve you in a minute. The rest of you, stand down for now.
Fucking useless detector is not working...
They had the high ground and still couldn’t take us... We’re damn good.
Don’t get cocky, rookie. That was all me.
Oh, I see. Much appreciated, Dogo.
Viper! We’ve got your guy — caught him a while back. Yellow jacket, right? Sending you our coordinates.
We’re on our way! Don’t take your eyes off him for a second — he’s dangerous. Remember what we talked about. Viktor, come here. Let’s talk while we move. Get ready.
Take your time — we’ve got him at gunpoint and he’s unarmed. He’s not going anywhere.
Are these the ones we were looking for? They were alive just a few minutes ago...
They don’t look right. You don’t need to be a scientist to see that...
Shit... I warned them not to underestimate him. Rookie, Dogo — cover the door. Rooster, you’re on the yard. Viktor! With me — let’s find anything that points to Atreo.
Copy that, Captain. Repositioning to the door!

A PDA with a voice note. I’ll play it: "Leto pulled some kind of trick and broke free. Knocked the guys out with a single motion of his hand — didn’t even touch them! You were right about your theory. I’m only still conscious because of this helmet... Take blockers before you get close. Get us out of this hell."

Well, that confirms what I told you before, Viktor. The artifact Leto’s carrying lets him manipulate PSI fields — that’s why we’ve run into these zombies more than once. And I’ve got another suspicion...
So that explains the zombies I saw earlier in the swamps... What now? How are we even supposed to track him?
Quiet! The damn radio’s acting up. Listen.
We’ve picked up the infidel’s signal— (static) —heading toward Dark Valley— (static) —he’s using the artifact to evade us, over.
That confirms it. Somehow, the Monolith are tracking Leto through the artifact. It’s dangerous, but we have to follow him to Dark Valley... Move out!
Something’s wrong... too quiet.
The mutants must’ve heard all the gunfire earlier.
Hold it! Monolith on the bridge. Keep your heads down as you move. Dogo—that includes you. Don’t make a scene again.
Alright. Nothing to worry about. I’ll behave...
We’re almost there. There’s an old tunnel we can use to cut straight into the Dark Valley. If we’re lucky, it won’t be crawling with anomalies. That should help us close the gap with Atreus.
We take whatever shortcut we need. We can’t lose him again.
If we give him any more distance, the place will be swarming with damn Monoliths by the time we get there.
WHAT THE HELL IS THAT!?
It’s a pseudogiant, rookie. Everyone—get ready.
Why the hell is it lifting its leg? What is it doing!? (scared)
Stay sharp. Check your gear—make sure you didn’t lose anything—and let’s move toward the drain up ahead.
(Breathing hard) Damn... I’m not twenty anymore…
How old are you, Dogo?
Forty-nine years of pure testosterone.
(Low chuckles)
What about you guys?
Thirty-seven.
Thirty-two. Still young.
Damn... And you, Viktor? How old are you?
Twenty-two...
(Surprised) Shit, I’m the youngest in the group.
Watch your step. Check where you’re putting your feet—I haven’t been through this route in years...
So... where are we heading now?
We’re moving to the farm southwest of the Valley. Stay sharp—I’ve got a feeling we’ll run into more Monolith.
Hope I’ve got enough buckshot left... running low.
I’ve got some in reserve. Don’t worry, Rookie.
Sweep the area. Make sure it’s secure.
There was a fight here not long ago...
These bandits are fresh. Stay alert.
Please, don’t shoot! I’ll talk!
You’d better start talking. And don’t try anything—I’m not in the mood today.
Stuff a sock in his mouth!
Pffft (laughter)

I heard you made it into the Dark Valley alive, and that you’re looking to speak with Sultan. Turns out we’ve got some shared interests, and I’ve arranged for him to see you—if you help him with a small matter. Tell him I sent you, Viktor. And stop by the bunker sometime—I’ve got new gear...
Now I look like a bandit.
Now I don’t look like a bandit anymore.
...What the hell!?
Who’s there? If you’re a mutant—get lost!
I’m not a mutant...
Come out. I’m here to get you out.
There’s no one here!
Clear the mutants first! Then I’ll come out!
Mutants? Shit…
Alright, it’s clear—come down!
Whew, thank God. I’m coming—just give me a second to grab my stuff.
Stay close to me—this place is crawling with crap.
Alright, sticking with you. Please don’t run off—I can’t keep up.
Watch out! ON YOUR RIGHT!
Shit! More are coming. I’m gonna die today... I’M GONNA DIE TODAY!
I don’t want to die :c
Oh no! More of them!
Nice! Give ’em hell—watch out, more incoming!
Shit, shit—SHIT! I never should’ve come to this cursed place.
Don’t die! I can’t handle them on my own!”
Watch out! ON YOUR RIGHT!
Stalker, over here! They’re about to eat me alive—I need help!
Stalker, I owe you one. Drop by if you need stitching up—or if you want a drink.
You’d better be the one buying...
Cocky rookies... thinking people will just do whatever they say. I’ll tag along, kid. Actually—you’re tagging along with me. You lead the way, I lead my people.
Whatever. Let’s move.
Alright, boys—rally at the Truck Cemetery entrance. We’re taking what’s ours.
Good. Everyone here? I’m talking to you, Scrap—always late.
Yeah, I’m here. Early this time.
We’ve got reinforcements from Kolyan, so don’t embarrass me, you bunch of idiots. We clear the area and retake the outpost where they killed our people.
(Excited murmurs)
Drinks are on me after. Only for the ones who make it. Heh—but first, we take the point
I see them! Take positions—plant your damn feet!
Watch it, there’s a lot of them!
Easy. Any losses? Meh… doesn’t matter. We keep moving.
Is there a prize for most kills?
Of course. It’ll be me—or maybe this guy Kolyan sent. He looks solid.
What’s the prize?
Naturally—MY FUCKING SCAR. Day after tomorrow I’m getting a shotgun I’ve wanted for a while.
(Whoops and cheers — bandits celebrating)
Drop the bottles—we’re almost there! This time there’s more of them!
Damn... I need a drink. Remember, drinks are on me later—don’t die!
One last point—follow me if you’ve got the guts!
Now we go for our boys. Maybe we’ll find their loot after we wipe these damn cultists—HAHA!
More coming at nine o’clock! Hold your ground!
Alright, let’s take a breather. The outpost is ours anyway. Your name was Viktor, right? Come—let’s talk.
Alright, on my way.
You look eager to check those bodies. Go ahead—we’ll talk after.
(Low chuckles)
(Injured)
(Injured) Another scar...
(Injured) I’ll turn the other cheek. Go ahead—take another shot if you’ve got the guts...
(Injured) Shit!
(Injured) Damn it... my arm...
Useless "fighters". It’s just you and me now, Viktor. I’ve always said—if you want something done right, do it yourself.
Now then... you son of a bitch. Right where I wanted you...
Dead Monolith... Could these be the ones following Atreus?
More bodies... He must be close.
... I suggest you turn back while you still can.
I’m not going anywhere. You and I have unfinished business.
(Maniacal laughter)
—What the hell is happening!?
I’m very busy right now. Don’t interrupt me.
Enough with the tricks! Show yourself, you bastard!
I don’t think you understand what you’re chasing. That artifact will only bring you trouble—a lot of trouble. You’re better off leaving it to me...
Do you know why I had to flee the Cordon? It wasn’t because of you... I was being hunted.
Of course you were. You stole from everyone.
(Mocking laughter) You have no idea what’s really going on.
I don’t give a damn. I’m going to find you anyway...
Stop hiding, you piece of shit!
How curious... Hiding is impossible at this point. And I’m not talking about you...
You’re stubborn. Doesn’t look like you’re going to stop anytime soon... Can’t you see I’m busy!?
Enough of the mind games! Stop trying to get into my head—GIVE ME THE ARTIFACT!
I’m starting to understand why you were chosen. Besides being hard-headed, your resistance to psi emissions is remarkably high... I can’t hold the door much longer.
Where are you!?
I’m not coming out that easily. You’ll have to find me—and when you do, it’ll already be too late.
I don’t know why you’re stalling. I’ll find you anyway—and that artifact will be mine.
Are you sure? I thought the same… others did too. You’re no different from us.
What others are you talking about!?
(Laughter)
I didn’t think you’d make it this far… WITH SO LITTLE LEFT.
So little left for what!?
ALMOST THERE! THREE MORE MINUTES!
Three minutes for what!?
(Maniacal laughter)
(Laughs) What’s wrong? Can’t move?
Sova... you bitch. I should’ve killed you when I had the chance.
Sova? Who is he talking to?
Ozero! I hope that anomaly burned you to ashes!
I don’t know who these people are... He’s insane. Talking to himself.
SHUT UP, YOU’RE BREAKING MY FOCUS!
Nox! You ruined my life, you bastard. I hope you never walk among the living again.
Nox!? What do you know about Nox?
You’ll regret this! All of you will regret this!
This can’t be… It rejected me. What do I do now?
Now you’re going to pay. That’s what.
Viktor. A man came asking for you—says he wants an artifact. Calls himself Kolyan. He’s waiting for you...
He’s armed.
